title: [Feature Request] Shift-click to select a range of records
author: Jordy-A
body: In the admin interface, it would be very helpful if selecting row 1, holding the Shift key, and then selecting row 10 would select all rows between 1 and 10. This would massively simplify deleting multiple records from the admin interface, and using the Shift key to select a range of items is common UX behaviour in list views. It would be a great feature to have.

title: [Feature Request] Add type date
author: oSethoum
body: I have a very specific case where i need to count daily engagements per product, and i need to have DATE type so i can have a `unique index` between product_id and the date.

title: Feature Request - Export collections in Markdown or TOON format
author: Gian-f
body: When sharing a PocketBase schema with AI tools, the JSON export can become quite large and includes a lot of data that is not always needed. It would be useful to have an export option focused on schema readability and lower token usage, such as Markdown or TOON. For example: ```bash pocketbase export collections --format=markdown ``` or: ```bash pocketbase export collections --format=toon ``` The export could include collection names, fields, relations, indexes, validation rules, and API rules, while skipping unnecessary metadata by default. Markdown would be useful for documentation and manual review. TOON would be useful for sending schemas to AI tools with less token overhead.

title: Footgun: The ?= operator with authentication data becomes a null checker
author: pernydev
body:
Hey!

I recently observed that a rule of mine, where I check `collaborators.id ?= @request.auth.id` was actually fully open to the public, as long as there is no authentication in the request. 

I traced this through the code, and found that `record_field_resolver_runner.go` line 201 simply resolves `@request.auth.id` to `"NULL"` when no auth is passed.
```
if r.resolver.requestInfo == nil || r.resolver.requestInfo.Auth == nil || r.resolver.requestInfo.Auth.Collection() == nil {
    return &search.ResolverResult{Identifier: "NULL"}, nil
}
```

This ends up meaning that the left side of the query becomes a `IS NULL` causing rows with no collaborators to be truthful. 

This is inherently not wrong behavior, it's just a hidden footgun I observed. When the docs describe `?=` as "Any/At least one of Equal", it doesn't make this kind of behavior together with `@request.auth.id` clear.

title: Can't change max select via Admin UI
author: svenkubiak
body:
PocketBase version: v039.2
Browser: Google Chrome Version 149.0.7827.54

Steps:
1. Create a collection with a file field.
2. Set maxSelect to 10.
3. Save.
4. Reopen the field settings.
5. Change maxSelect from 10 to 5.
6. Leave the input / trigger onchange.

Result:
The browser console shows:
Uncaught ReferenceError: props is not defined at HTMLInputElement.onchange

The changed maxSelect value is not persisted / the save button remains disabled.

title: If S3 upload fails during backup, the backup file is not created / available in the Admin-UI
author: mgruel
body: I have configured the backup function to use S3. Now i have the issue, when the upload to the S3-Service fails due to an HTTP-Error, the backup file vanishes and is not longer visible in the Admin-UI. Example of an error: ``` [key: @auto_pb_backup_xxx_20260607070000.zip] multipart upload error: 502 BadGateway: The server was unable to complete your request. (RAW: BadGateway The server was unable to complete your request. N/A N/A ) ```

title: Manually changing a date immediately marks it as invalid
author: Jordy-A
body: In the PocketBase admin, when I go into an existing record, and modify a date, it immediately marks it as invalid. Eg, when a date is set as `05/06/2026, 12:46:14` and then I press the `06` to make it `05`, it immediately resets it to `dd/mm/yyyy, --:--:--` once I press the `0`. Could you please either: - don't auto-reset invalid dates to `dd/mm/yyyy, --:--:--` but just show an error state below it - add some sort of delay before it marks the date as invalid It would make adjusting dates much faster.

title: Keyboard Shortcut to Duplicate/Create New Records

title: Sort by date column type in UI shows sort incorrectly
author: a200xeaf
body: PocketBase v0.39.0 im not sure the exact cause but sorting by date column in collections with more than ~24 records causes the UI to show them sorted incorrectly. from what i can tell the api is returning correctly but the UI is visually incorrect. see the image below where "test" column is sorted but the dates are out of order. this is on a new collection with no hooks and no other dependencies. when i reverse the sort without refreshing the same issue is present and the sort does not correct itself

title: Feature Request: Add a readonly property for the field
author: VitalyV
body:
Hi.

On one project, I needed the opportunity to make the `IsDeleted` property as a read-only field in the PocketBase.

Now in the project I can make a field `hidden` and it will be available for management in the admin panel, but will not be accessible through the API.

But sometimes, the opposite situation may be needed, when working with a field is available through the API, but in the admin panel you could only see its value and build filters based on it.

Оne of the cases when this is useful. 
My PWA application uses RxDB, which is very interesting for working with elements that have been deleted.
It is not possible to restore a deleted item in RxDB itself, but it remains in the database for correct data replication. And if, after replication, the deleted item is set `IsDeleted = false` in the admin panel, it will not restore the old item, it will create a duplicate.
I understand that this is a problem with a specific database and its implementation, but it is in such a case that it will be very useful to prevent the user from changing the value for the `IsDeleted` field in the admin panel.

I also think that this can be added for all fields in PocketBase.
Thanks
